name: Delete POP
description: The Delete POP removes (or keeps) points or primitives using 5 methods found on the Attribute, Thin, Pattern, Group and Bounding pages. You can choose to delete points or primitives. On the Attribute page you can delete based on the values of point or primitive attributes. On the Thin page you can delete based on parameter-defined ranges, steps or randomly. You can delete based on patterns using [[Pattern Matching]] on the Pattern page. You can delete based on point or primitive groups on the Group page. You can delete based on bounding ranges on any attribute on the Bounding page. Four of the methods let you build the logic using sequential blocks of parameters. For example on the Attribute page, one block selects P(0) > 0 and another that selects P(1) < 0 and ANDed together they will delete points in a quarter of the space. Otherwise is common to build an attribute in a [[Math Mix POP]] and use it in the Delete POP. When deleting primitives, if points or vertices are no longer used, they are deleted. When deleting points, the vertices and primitives that use them can be deleted in part or entirely. Deleted points can cause like strips to be split, trimmed or deleted entirely. For pattern matching on the Pattern page, see [[Pattern Matching]]. Note: Deleting attributes can be done with the [[Attribute POP]], the [[Select POP]] and all the Math POPs. See also [[Group POP]]. The [[Field POP]] also can delete points.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.label: Operation
parameters.description: Inverts the selection of elements being deleted.

parameters.name: Entity
parameters.label: Entity
parameters.description: Sets the attribute class to use.

parameters.name: Line Strip Behavior
parameters.label: Line Strip Behavior
parameters.description: What to do when points of a line strip are deleted.

parameters.name: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.label: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.description: Enable copying the point count and topology information held on the GPU to the CPU.

parameters.name: Attribute
parameters.label: Attribute
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks to create new attributes.

parameters.name: Enabled
parameters.label: Enabled
parameters.description: Enable thinning by index range, index step or random index.

parameters.name: Thin Range Start
parameters.label: Thin Range Start
parameters.description: Determines the starting index for range-based point filtering.

parameters.name: Thin Range Length
parameters.label: Thin Range Length
parameters.description: Determines the number of points being filtered by index range.

parameters.name: Thin Random
parameters.label: Thin Random
parameters.description: Determines the proportion of points randomly filtered.

parameters.name: Thin Random Seed
parameters.label: Thin Random Seed
parameters.description: Sets the random seed for points being randomly filtered.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description: Invert the selection resulting from the current conditions in the block.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.label: Pattern
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for index-matching pattern.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Bound
parameters.label: Bound
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for bounding volumes.
